c74object.name: fluid.pca~
c74object.description: Principal Components Analysis (PCA) of a DataSet.

discussion.p: PCA fits to a DataSet to determine its principal components, each of which is a new axis through the data that maximises the variance, or “differences”, within the Data. PCA can then transform the original DataSet or individual points to position them in relation to the principal components (i.e., “new axes”) for better comparing how they differ from other points in the DataSet. PCA is often used for dimensionality reduction and is also useful for removing redundancy (i.e., correlation) and/or noise (i.e., dimensions that are uniformly distributed) from a DataSet.
Read more about fluid.pca~ on the learn platform.
fluid.pca~ is part of the Fluid Decomposition Toolkit of the FluCoMa project. For more explanations, learning material, and discussions on its musicianly uses, visit flucoma.org.

description.p: Name for this instance, so that it can be reused or referred to by other data objects. If none is given, a name will be generated

method.name: reset
method.digest: Return the object to its original state
method.description: Calling reset will change parameter values back to those the object was created with, i.e. any attribute values set in the box will be retained

method.name: refer
method.description: Changes the reference to the data in another named fluid.pca~ object. Changes to the data stored in any referenced will be shared by all other similarly named fluid.pca~ objects.

description.p: Train this model on a fluid.dataset~ to determine the principal components, but don't transform any data.

Given a trained model, transform a source fluid.dataset~ into the PCA-space and write to a destination DataSet. The DataSets can be the same for both input and output (performs the operation in-place). This process returns the fraction (between 0 and 1) of explained variance. The minimum number of points in the source DataSet should be equal or greater than numdimensions.
fit and transform in a single pass. Returns the fraction (between 0 and 1) of explained variance. The minimum number of points in the source DataSet should be equal or greater than numdimensions.

Given a trained model, invert a source fluid.dataset~ containing numdimensions dimensions that are in PCA-space to a destination fluid.dataset~ with the dimensionality of the data that was used to fit. fluid.dataset~ can be the same for both input and output (the operation will be performed in-place).
Given a trained model, transform the data point in sourcebuffer from the original dimensional space to numdimensions in PCA-space and write the result into destbuffer.
Given a trained model, transform the data point in sourcebuffer from being numdimensions in PCA-space into the original dimensional space and write into destbuffer.
The number of columns (dimensions) in this model or dataset / labeset
The number of data points (entries / observations) in this model or dataset / labeset
Resets the internal state of the model

method.name: load
method.description: Replace the internal state of the object from a dict. The message should take the form load dictionary <dictid>

method.name: dump
method.digest: Dump the state of this object from the dump outlet as a dict.

method.name: read
method.description: Replace the internal state of the object from a JSON file on disk. With no argument, a file dialog will appear to choose the file. It is always executed at the lowest priority on the main thread, using deferlow internally.

method.name: write
method.description: Save the internal state of the object to a JSON file on disk. With no argument, a file dialog will appear to choose the file. It is always executed at the lowest priority on the main thread, using deferlow internally.

description.p: The number of dimensions (principal components) to keep after a transform, using PCA for dimensionality reduction.
Enable warnings to be issued whenever a parameter value is constrained (e.g. clipped)
A flag (0 or 1) indicating whether or not to perform "whitening" on the output of PCA. For more information on whitening, visit https://learn.flucoma.org/reference/pca
